items.title: Infosecurity Europe
items.ai_summary: The provided threat item lacks specific technical details, making it challenging to assess its severity. However, given its categorization as 'news' and the absence of detected TTPs or a CVE ID, it appears to be more informational than a direct cybersecurity threat. The impact, if any, would likely be limited to the dissemination of information that could potentially be used in social engineering attacks or for reconnaissance purposes. To mitigate such risks, organizations should ensure their staff are aware of the potential for misinformation and are trained to verify the authenticity of news sources. Regular security awareness training can help in this regard.

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: The Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, leveraging the T1195.001 tactic to compromise software dependencies. This threat actor has also published three additional malicious npm packages, @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils, which contain infostealer code and have collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads. If exploited, these packages could lead to unauthorized data access and theft, affecting developers and organizations that have installed these packages. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the affected packages and update their dependencies to the latest versions, ensuring that their npm packages are up-to-date and secure. Additionally, developers should verify the authenticity of packages before installation, and consider using tools like npm audit to identify potential vulnerabilities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195.001| B[Compromise Software Dependencies]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Active Supply Chain Attack Compromises Packages on npm
items.description: An active npm supply chain attack has compromised packages in the @antv ecosystem, affecting the maintainer account 'atool'. The attack is part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involving 639 compromised package versions across 323 unique packages. Notable affected packages include echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads, and widely-used @antv packages for data visualization. The malwar
items.ai_summary: The active supply chain attack on npm has compromised 639 package versions across 323 unique packages in the @antv ecosystem, including echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads. The attack, part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involves the maintainer account 'atool' and utilizes the T1195 supply chain compromise tactic. If exploited, this could lead to malicious code execution in applications using the compromised packages, potentially affecting millions of users. To remediate, developers should immediately remove and update affected packages to the latest versions, ensuring they are using secure and verified dependencies. Additionally, npm users should monitor their accounts for suspicious activity and consider implementing package signing and verification to prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Tooling Seen in Real-World Intrusion
items.description: Activity involving BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend tooling from the Nightmare-Eclipse proof-of-concept repository was observed during a live intrusion investigation. The malicious binaries were staged in user-writable directories including Pictures and Downloads folders, with execution attempts failing despite hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities. The threat actor demonstrated unfamiliarit
items.ai_summary: The Nightmare-Eclipse tooling, comprising BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, has been observed in real-world intrusions, with malicious binaries staged in user-writable directories such as Pictures and Downloads folders. The threat actor's execution attempts were unsuccessful, despite demonstrating hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities, suggesting a potential lack of familiarity with the tooling. To mitigate this threat, defenders should monitor user-writable directories for suspicious activity and restrict execution of binaries from these locations. Additionally, implementing a least-privilege approach and regularly reviewing system logs can help detect and prevent similar intrusions. Defenders should also consider updating their endpoint detection and response tools to include signatures for the Nightmare-Eclipse tooling.

    C -->|T1112| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 9 Year-Old PHP Vulnerability Keeps Swinging As One of the Most Targeted Vulnerabilities
items.description: CVE-2017-9841, a remote code execution vulnerability in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, remains highly exploited nearly a decade after disclosure. Analysis reveals over 80,000 exploitation attempts detected in 30 days, with 36,500 hits in the last 10 days alone. The vulnerability affects PHPUnit versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code via POST
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2017-9841 vulnerability is a remote code execution flaw in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, which has been highly exploited nearly a decade after its disclosure. This vulnerability affects PHPUnit versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code via a POST request. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access and control of the affected system, with potential real-world impacts including data breaches and ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their PHPUnit versions to at least 4.8.28 or 5.6.3, and restrict access to the eval-stdin.php file. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent exploitation attempts.
items.workflow_graph:

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Exposing Fox Tempest: A malware-signing service operation
items.description: Fox Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor operating a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) business used by cybercriminals to distribute malicious code, including ransomware. The actor abuses Microsoft Artifact Signing to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates, allowing malware to evade security controls. Fox Tempest created over a thousand certificates and established hundreds of
items.ai_summary: The Fox Tempest threat actor is operating a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) business, which has been used to distribute malicious code, including ransomware, by abusing Microsoft Artifact Signing to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates. This allows the malware to evade security controls, with over a thousand certificates created and hundreds of command and control servers established. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for widespread data encryption and ransomware attacks, affecting various organizations and individuals. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict certificate validation, monitor for suspicious certificate usage, and ensure that all systems are updated with the latest security patches, specifically focusing on Microsoft's certificate validation mechanisms. Additionally, defenders should prioritize network segmentation and implement robust backup and disaster recovery procedures to mitigate the impact of potential ransomware attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Latest PyPi Compromise
items.description: A supply chain attack targeting the Microsoft DurableTask Python client compromised versions 1.4.1, 1.4.2, and 1.4.3 on PyPi. The threat actor gained access through a compromised GitHub account previously linked to attacks, using stolen credentials to dump GitHub secrets containing PyPi tokens. The evolved payload targets Linux systems, stealing credentials from AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, Vault,
items.ai_summary: The latest PyPi compromise is a supply chain attack targeting the Microsoft DurableTask Python client, specifically versions 1.4.1, 1.4.2, and 1.4.3. The threat actor gained access through a compromised GitHub account, utilizing stolen credentials to dump GitHub secrets containing PyPi tokens, which is an example of T1078 (Valid Accounts) and T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise). This evolved payload targets Linux systems, stealing credentials from various cloud services including AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, and Vault, resulting in significant real-world impact. To remediate, defenders should immediately update to a version outside the affected range, such as version 1.4.4 or later, and review their GitHub account security, ensuring two-factor authentication is enabled and monitoring for suspicious activity. Additionally, rotating credentials for affected services and implementing a secrets management solution like HashiCorp Vault can help mitigate the attack's impact.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1078| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1195| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside Banana RAT: From Build Server to Banking Fraud
items.description: An MDR investigation successfully mapped the complete operational infrastructure of Banana RAT, a Brazilian banking trojan operated by threat cluster SHADOW-WATER-063. The investigation uncovered both server-side and client-side components, revealing a sophisticated FastAPI-based polymorphic payload generation system that produces hash-unique builds to evade detection. The malware employs layered
items.ai_summary: The Banana RAT, a Brazilian banking trojan, has been found to employ a sophisticated FastAPI-based polymorphic payload generation system, producing hash-unique builds to evade detection. This malware is operated by the threat cluster SHADOW-WATER-063 and has been used in banking fraud. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detection of TTP T1190, and impairing defenses, as seen with T1562. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls and ensure that all public-facing applications are up-to-date and securely configured, specifically focusing on input validation and secure coding practices to prevent exploitation. Additionally, implementing a robust endpoint detection and response system can help in early detection and mitigation of such threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1562| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Popular Go Decimal Library Targeted by Long-Running Typosquat with DNS Backdoor
items.description: A long-running typosquatting campaign impersonated the widely used shopspring/decimal Go library by publishing github.com/shopsprint/decimal, differing by a single character. Active since November 2017, the package remained benign through seven releases until being weaponized in August 2023 with version v1.3.3. This version introduced a malicious init() function that executes automatically on impo
items.ai_summary: The shopspring/decimal Go library has been targeted by a long-running typosquatting campaign, where an attacker published a malicious package on github.com/shopsprint/decimal, differing by a single character. The package remained benign until version v1.3.3 in August 2023, which introduced a malicious init() function that executes automatically on import, potentially allowing for remote access. This vulnerability affects any Go project that imported the malicious package, and exploitation could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the malicious package and update their dependencies to the legitimate shopspring/decimal library, ensuring they are using a version prior to v1.3.3 or a patched version if available. Additionally, defenders should monitor their DNS traffic for suspicious activity and consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: macOS ClickFix Campaign: AppleScript Stealers & New Terminal Protections
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign targets both Windows and macOS users through fake CAPTCHA pages that trick victims into executing malicious commands. The macOS variant deploys an AppleScript-based infostealer that harvests sensitive data including keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies from 12 browsers, over 200 browser extensions, and 16 cryptocurrency wallets. The malware employs
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign targets macOS users through fake CAPTCHA pages, tricking them into executing malicious AppleScript commands, which deploy an infostealer that harvests sensitive data from keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies. The malware affects macOS users who execute the malicious AppleScript, potentially leading to significant data breaches. To remediate, defenders should update their macOS systems to the latest version, ensure that Gatekeeper is enabled to restrict application downloads, and configure their systems to only allow trusted AppleScript sources. Additionally, users should be cautious when encountering CAPTCHA pages and avoid executing unknown scripts. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, as it can lead to the theft of sensitive data, including cryptocurrency wallet credentials.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.002| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1176| C[Collection]:::tactic
    C -->|T1539| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1555| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Void Dokkaebi Uses Fake Job Interview Lure to Spread Malware via Code Repositories
items.description: Void Dokkaebi, also known as Famous Chollima, has evolved its operations into a self-propagating supply chain threat targeting software developers. The North Korea-aligned group uses fabricated job interviews to lure developers into cloning malicious repositories. Once compromised, the victim's machine becomes an infection vector through two mechanisms: malicious VS Code task configurations that e
items.ai_summary: The Void Dokkaebi threat actor, also known as Famous Chollima, has been observed using a fake job interview lure to spread malware via code repositories, targeting software developers. This self-propagating supply chain threat compromises victim machines through malicious VS Code task configurations, allowing the attackers to gain control over the infected systems. The attack vector involves the use of fabricated job interviews to trick developers into cloning malicious repositories, which then execute malicious code, potentially leading to a supply chain compromise (T1195) and user execution (T1204) of malware. To remediate this threat, developers should be cautious when cloning repositories from unknown sources and should inspect the repository's code and configurations carefully, especially VS Code task configurations, before executing them. Additionally, email accounts (T1586.002) should be secured with strong passwords and multi-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1586.002| D[Email Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2025-61311: A reflected cross-site scripted (XSS) vulnerability in the dfm-menu_alerts.php c...
items.description: A reflected cross-site scripted (XSS) vulnerability in the dfm-menu_alerts.php component of GmbH Mecury Managed Print Services (docuForm) v11.11c allows attackers to execute arbitrary Javascript in the context of a user's browser via injecting a crafted payload into an unfiltered variable value.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-61311 is a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the dfm-menu_alerts.php component of GmbH Mecury Managed Print Services (docuForm) v11.11c. Attackers can inject a crafted payload into an unfiltered variable value, allowing them to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of a user's browser. This vulnerability can lead to session hijacking, data theft, and unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate, update to docuForm v11.12a or later, and ensure the dfm-menu_alerts.php component is properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks. Additionally, implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block suspicious traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

This vulnerability allows a remote attacker to achieve arbitrary command execution on the host by exploiting unsanitized user-controlled parameters within crafted links in the system logs user interface (UI). An attacker can inject shell metacharacters and command substitutions into these parameters, leading to the execution of arbitrary shell commands on the affected

items.title: Selective HTTP Proxying in Linux, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: Recently, Rob wrote about a tool, Proxifier, that can intercept requests from specific processes. Proxifier is available for Windows, macOS, and Android. But I have not seen a generic Linux option yet. The advantage of a tool like Proxifier is the ability to target specific software. For debugging, reverse engineering, and similar tasks, selecting a specific process is quite useful, as it creates less noise to sift through and simplifies analysis.

items.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
items.description: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
items.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
items.description: This week starts small. A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust. That is what makes it worrying. The danger is in normal things now - updates, apps, cloud buttons, support chats, trusted accounts. AI

items.title: Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform
items.description: The company has developed a platform that uses specialized AI agents to inspect every incoming message. The post Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention

items.description: Flipper Devices, the maker of the Flipper Zero pentesting tool, is asking the community to help build Flipper One, an open Linux platform for connected devices. [...]

items.title: Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking
items.description: CVE-2026-9082 can be exploited without authentication for information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution. The post Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
items.description: Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild. The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the CVSS scoring system. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges. "Improper link resolution before file access ('link following') in Microsoft Defender

items.title: Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation
items.description: The company will invest in its firewall, certified patches, protection extensions, new products, and team expansion. The post Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
items.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one misconfigured anything or violated a policy. Yet that single key, which was easily accessible to a minor-league attacker, could have opened a path to some 98% of entities in the company's cloud

items.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days
items.description: The bugs could be exploited to elevate privileges to System or create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI
items.description: More than 200 vulnerabilities patched in recent Chrome releases are marked as ‘reported by Google’. The post Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility
items.description: New vulnerabilities are being discovered too fast, the time-to-exploitation is too short, and our visibility into them is largely lacking. The post Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft warns of new Defender zero-days exploited in attacks
items.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft started rolling out security patches for two Defender vulnerabilities that have been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a case of improper privilege management that could permit an unprivileged local user to disclose sensitive files and execute arbitrary commands as root on default installations of several major

items.title: GitHub links repo breach to TanStack npm supply-chain attack
items.description: GitHub says the hackers who breached 3,800 internal repositories gained access via a malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, compromised in last week's TanStack npm supply-chain attack. [...]

items.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
items.description: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension.  The development comes as the Nx team revealed that the extension, nrwl.angular-console, was breached after one of its developers' systems was hacked in the

items.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
items.description: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or information disclosure. The vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0, per CVE.org. Drupal said the vulnerability resides in a database abstraction API that is

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 21st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9940, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Europe dismantles VPN service used by cybercriminals to hide ransomware attacks
items.description: The international operation targeted a service known as First VPN, which had been marketed for years on Russian-speaking cybercrime forums as a secure way for criminals to evade law enforcement.

items.title: Xi and Putin pledge closer cooperation on AI, cyberspace and satellite systems
items.description: In a lengthy joint statement, Moscow and Beijing pledged closer cooperation on satellite internet technologies and joint work on software development and open-source initiatives — part of a broader effort to reduce reliance on Western technology and build a more independent technological ecosystem capable of competing with countries both states consider “unfriendly.”

items.title: Ukraine identifies infostealer operator tied to 28,000 stolen accounts
items.description: The Ukrainian cyberpolice, working in conjunction with U.S. law enforcement, has identified an 18-year-old man from Odesa suspected of running an infostealer malware operation targeting users of an online store in California. [...]

items.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
items.description: Threat actors brute-forced VPN credentials and bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA) on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances to deploy tools used in ransomware attacks. [...]

items.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.description: Open source software giant GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of repos. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
items.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: Processes and Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
items.description: Government leaders revealed that, in spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents showed issues persist and visibility falls short.

items.title: FTC warns 12 major tech firms of violating Take It Down Act
items.description: The law mandates that platforms make it easy for people to ask that nonconsensual intimate images be removed and to delete them within 48 hours of a request.

items.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
items.description: Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents. RAMPART, short for Risk Assessment and Measurement Platform for Agentic Red Teaming, functions as a Pytest-native safety and security testing framework for writing and running safety and security tests for AI agents, covering

items.title: Ukraine probes teen suspect in cyber theft scheme targeting California online shoppers
items.description: The investigation began after U.S. authorities informed their Ukrainian counterparts that hackers operating from Ukraine could be involved in attacks targeting users of American e-commerce platforms, Ukraine's Prosecutor General said.

items.title: Discord migrates all users to end-to-end encryption by default
items.description: The move comes as other major social media platforms are killing end-to-end encryption for messaging. In recent months, Instagram and TikTok both announced they will no longer offer the feature.

items.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
items.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

items.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
items.description: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]

items.title: Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution
items.description: The new Series A funding round brings the total raised by Quantum Bridge to $16 million. The post Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
items.description: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. The tech giant attributed the activity to a threat actor it calls Fox Tempest, which it said offered the MSaaS scheme

items.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
items.description: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verification. [...]

items.title: Drupal critical update to fix bug with high exploitation risk
items.description: Drupal has announced a "core security release" scheduled for later today, warning that threat actors might develop exploits within hours of the update disclosure. [...]

items.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&C) communications. Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in September 2022, is assessed to be active since at least 2022, targeting government agencies

items.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
items.description: New Industry Data Just Released Suggests Not. On May 19th, 2026, Orchid Security released the results of our Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026. Among the findings, "identity dark matter" (the unseen, unmanaged elements of identity) now overshadows the visible elements 57% vs. 43%. And it couldn't have occurred at a worse time, with enterprises embracing Agent AI with both arms (and unfortunately, as

items.title: Exploit released for new PinTheft Arch Linux root escalation flaw
items.description: PinTheft, a recently patched Linux privilege escalation vulnerability, now has a publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that allows local attackers to gain root privileges on Arch Linux systems. [...]

items.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
items.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.

items.title: What It'll Take to Make AI BOMs Usable in a Modern Security Program
items.description: Five ways CISOs can prepare for consuming AI Bill of Materials and influence the direction of how they're generated.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
items.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs).

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 19th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9936, (Tue, May 19th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9932, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
